title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jaundice
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jaundice are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs that are used to treat this liver condition. They help with jaundice by supporting liver function, promoting bile flow, and reducing inflammation. Some examples of herbal tinctures that are commonly used for jaundice include milk thistle, dandelion root, and turmeric. These herbs contain compounds that protect liver cells, stimulate bile production, and have anti-inflammatory properties. By using these herbal tinctures, patients with jaundice can experience improved liver function, reduced bilirubin levels, and an overall improvement in their quality of life. For instance, a patient with jaundice who uses milk thistle tincture may notice a decrease in itching and fatigue, as well as an improvement in their skin tone and appetite.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tincture helps with jaundice because it promotes the production of bile and its flow from the liver, which is crucial for the elimination of bilirubin from the body. Bilirubin is a yellow pigment that accumulates in the blood and tissues, causing the characteristic yellowing of the skin and eyes in jaundice. Dandelion is also known for its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the liver from further damage and support its healthy functioning. Furthermore, it contains taraxacum, a compound that has been shown to improve liver health and reduce the symptoms of jaundice. Therefore, dandelion tincture is a natural and effective remedy for jaundice, promoting liver health and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Diuretic: Dandelion tincture promotes urination, which helps eliminate bilirubin from the body, a key component in reducing jaundice symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of taraxacin and taraxacerin.

Cholagogue: It aids in stimulating the flow of bile from the liver, which is essential for jaundice treatment as it helps remove bilirubin. The active constituents responsible for this are taraxacoside and cichoric acid.

Hepatoprotective: Dandelion tincture protects the liver, which is crucial in jaundice management as it helps maintain liver function and health. This is attributed mainly to its antioxidant properties and the presence of vitamins C and E.

Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for jaundice as it can help alleviate liver inflammation often associated with the condition. The anti-inflammatory effects are due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, in particular, taraxasterol.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tincture has antioxidant properties, which are vital for jaundice treatment as they help protect liver cells from oxidative stress and support overall liver health. The antioxidant properties are attributed to various compounds, including vitamins A, C, and E, and phenolic acids such as chlorogenic acid.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions contain compounds that support liver function, making them useful for treating jaundice.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, dandelion leaves help promote bile production and have anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in jaundice treatment.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and flavonoids, dandelion flowers support liver function and help reduce inflammation, which is beneficial for jaundice.
Buds: Dandelion buds are known for their detoxifying properties, which help remove toxins from the body and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain oils and compounds that support liver health, making them useful for treating jaundice and other liver-related conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried dandelion roots and leaves according to recipe requirements for 20% herb-to-alcohol ratio.
Chop the gathered materials into small pieces to facilitate soaking in high-proof vodka at room temperature overnight.
Transfer the chopped material mixture to a clean glass jar leaving one inch of headspace and add 80 proof vodka as needed.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks shaking daily with gentle pressure.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids after allowing mixture to drip completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with jaundice because it contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the buildup of bilirubin in the body. Curcumin helps to reduce bilirubin levels by stimulating the production of bile in the liver, which aids in the elimination of bilirubin from the body. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to protect liver cells from damage, promoting overall liver health and function. These properties make turmeric tincture a valuable natural remedy for supporting liver function and alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of curcumin, help reduce inflammation in the liver, aiding in jaundice treatment.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of turmeric tincture, also from curcumin, combats oxidative stress in the liver, promoting healing in jaundice.
Cholagogue: Turmeric tincture stimulates bile production and flow, provided by curcumin and other curcuminoids, benefiting jaundice patients by assisting in bile duct function.
Hepatoprotective: Turmeric tincture's hepatoprotective properties, attributable to curcumin, shield the liver from damage, supporting recovery in jaundice.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial effects of turmeric tincture, owing to curcumin and other curcuminoids, help prevent infections in jaundice, which can exacerbate the condition.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of turmeric, rich in curcumin, is commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for jaundice due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The fibrous root system of turmeric contains curcuminoids, which have been shown to improve liver function, making it beneficial for jaundice treatment.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine for jaundice, as they contain essential oils with hepatoprotective properties that can help protect the liver.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers, although less commonly used, contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds, which can aid in detoxification and support liver health in jaundice patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried turmeric roots weighing approximately two pounds.
Chop the turmeric roots into small pieces and combine with one cup of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in an amber glass bottle with a dropper lid and keep it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry tincture helps with jaundice because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has a proven antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effect. Jaundice is often caused by an infection or inflammation of the liver, bile ducts, or pancreas. Berberine can help reduce inflammation in these organs, thus alleviating the symptoms of jaundice. Moreover, barberry has been shown to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats and the elimination of waste from the body. By promoting the flow of bile, barberry tincture can help remove the excess bilirubin that causes jaundice, making it an effective natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue: Barberry tincture stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to eliminate bilirubin, a pigment that accumulates in the blood in jaundice. This property is due to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine.

Hepatoprotective: Barberry tincture protects the liver tissue, which is crucial in jaundice as the condition is characterized by liver dysfunction. The hepatoprotective effect is attributed to the antioxidant properties of berberine.

Anti-inflammatory: Barberry tincture reduces inflammation, which is important in jaundice as it often involves inflammation of the liver. The anti-inflammatory effect is due to the presence of berberine and other alkaloids.

Antimicrobial: Barberry tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms, which is important in jaundice as it can be caused by bacterial infections. The antimicrobial effect is mainly due to the presence of berberine.

Antioxidant: Barberry tincture neutralizes free radicals, which is important in jaundice as liver dysfunction can lead to increased oxidative stress. The antioxidant effect is attributed to the presence of various compounds, including berberine and other alkaloids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of alkaloids, which are useful in treating jaundice by stimulating bile production and promoting liver function.
Bark: Rich in berberine, an active compound that has anti-inflammatory and liver-protecting properties, making it effective for jaundice treatment.
Berries: High in antioxidants, which help protect liver cells from damage and support the overall health of the liver in jaundice patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried berberis vulgaris rootstock in the fall season for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested rootstock into small pieces and combine with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tincture helps with jaundice because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have protective effects on the liver. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes, which occurs when there is an excess of bilirubin in the blood. Bilirubin is a yellowish substance produced by the liver when it breaks down old red blood cells. When the liver is damaged or not functioning properly, as in the case of jaundice, bilirubin can build up in the body. Silymarin in milk thistle tincture helps to support liver function and can help reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood, thereby alleviating the symptoms of jaundice. Additionally, milk thistle has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to protect liver cells from further damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the liver, a common symptom of jaundice, due to the presence of flavonoids like silymarin.

Hepatoprotective: The hepatoprotective nature of milk thistle tincture protects liver cells from damage, aiding in jaundice treatment, as it contains silymarin which inhibits toxin penetration into liver cells.
Antioxidant: Milk thistle tincture's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress in the liver, a contributing factor in jaundice, due to its high flavonoid and polyphenol content.

Regenerative: The regenerative properties of milk thistle tincture promote liver cell regeneration, beneficial for jaundice recovery, as silymarin stimulates protein synthesis necessary for liver cell repair.
Cholagogue: Milk thistle tincture's cholagogue properties help stimulate bile production and flow, alleviating jaundice symptoms, because silymarin facilitates the elimination of bile from the liver.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid that is known for its ability to protect and repair the liver, making it effective in treating jaundice.
Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice.
Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant contain bioflavonoids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on liver function, making them useful in the treatment of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh milk thistle flowers and leaves according to sustainable harvesting practices.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried milk thistle with two parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 weeks and then strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for at least 6 months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Katuki tincture helps with jaundice because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that support liver function. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats and the elimination of waste products from the body. By promoting the flow of bile, Katuki tincture helps to reduce the accumulation of bilirubin in the blood, a key characteristic of jaundice. Furthermore, Katuki tincture has been shown to protect liver cells from damage, promoting overall liver health and functioning. As a result, Katuki tincture is a valuable herbal remedy for supporting liver function and alleviating the symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue: Katuki tincture stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to eliminate bilirubin from the body. The active constituents responsible for this property are the iridoid glycosides, such as swertiamarin and sweroside.

Hepatoprotective: Katuki tincture protects the liver cells from damage, which is crucial in jaundice as it helps to prevent further liver dysfunction. The hepatoprotective effect is attributed to the presence of bitter principles like amarogentin and gentianine.

Anti-inflammatory: Katuki tincture reduces inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it alleviates symptoms and promotes healing. The anti-inflammatory property is due to the presence of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds.

Antioxidant: Katuki tincture has antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals, which is important for jaundice as it prevents oxidative stress and further liver damage. The antioxidant activity is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Diuretic: Katuki tincture promotes the production of urine, which is important for jaundice as it helps to eliminate waste products and toxins from the body. The diuretic effect is due to the presence of various compounds, including iridoid glycosides and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the katuki plant contain powerful bitter compounds that can help stimulate the flow of bile, making it useful for treating jaundice.
Leaves: The leaves of the katuki plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve jaundice symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the katuki plant contain flavonoids that have hepatoprotective properties, making them useful for treating liver disorders such as jaundice.
Seeds: The seeds of the katuki plant contain essential oils and alkaloids that can help improve liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried katuki roots and wash them thoroughly with clean water.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part katuki root material with two parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Andrographis tincture helps with jaundice because it has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support liver health. The active compounds in Andrographis, particularly andrographolide, are known to have hepatoprotective properties, which means they can protect the liver from damage and promote its recovery. Jaundice is often a symptom of an underlying liver issue, and Andrographis tincture may help by reducing inflammation, protecting liver cells, and improving bile flow, which can all contribute to the reduction of jaundice symptoms. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Hepatoprotective: Andrographis tincture helps protect the liver from damage, which is important for jaundice as it is a liver condition that causes a yellowing of the skin and whites of the eyes due to the buildup of bilirubin. The active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide and deoxyandrographolide.

Anti-inflammatory: Andrographis tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for jaundice as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling in the liver. The active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide and 14-deoxyandrographolide.

Choleretic: Andrographis tincture promotes the production of bile, which is important for jaundice as it can help improve the flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, and reduce the buildup of bilirubin. The active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide and deoxyandrographolide.

Antioxidant: Andrographis tincture has antioxidant properties, which is important for jaundice as it can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. The active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide and 14-deoxyandrographolide.

Antimicrobial: Andrographis tincture has antimicrobial properties, which is important for jaundice as it can help prevent or treat infections that can complicate jaundice. The active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide and deoxyandrographolide.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the andrographis plant are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for jaundice due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Leaves: The leaves of the andrographis plant are used in medicinal tinctures for jaundice because they contain high levels of andrographolide, a compound known for its liver-protecting and detoxifying effects.
Flowers: The flowers of the andrographis plant are used in medicinal tinctures for jaundice as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and protect liver cells.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh andrographis paniculata leaves in the early morning or late afternoon when they are most potent.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by rinsing them gently with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 100 grams of cleaned andrographis paniculata leaves with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the botanicals to extract their active compounds into the solvent.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, transferring it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stonebreaker tincture helps with jaundice because it promotes the flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, which aids in the elimination of waste and toxins from the body. This herbal remedy contains compounds that support liver function and can help reduce inflammation, which is often a contributing factor in jaundice. By improving the overall health and function of the liver, stonebreaker tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and promote a faster recovery. Additionally, stonebreaker tincture has been shown to protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals and other harmful substances, making it a valuable tool in the prevention and treatment of jaundice.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Stonebreaker tincture stimulates the production of bile, which is important for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin from the body. The active constituents responsible for this property are the iridoid glycosides, such as geniposide and sweroside.

Hepatoprotective property: Stonebreaker tincture protects the liver cells from damage, which is crucial in jaundice treatment as it helps regenerate and maintain liver function. The flavonoids, like rutin and quercetin, contribute to this hepatoprotective effect.

Antioxidant property: Stonebreaker tincture contains antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, important for jaundice management as it reduces liver injury and promotes healing. Diuretic property: Stonebreaker tincture promotes urination, which is beneficial for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin and other toxins from the body. The diuretic effect is due to the presence of potassium salts and other active constituents in the tincture.

Anti-inflammatory property: Stonebreaker tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for jaundice treatment as it alleviates symptoms and supports liver recovery. The anti-inflammatory effect is attributed to the iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and other phenolic compounds present in the tincture.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, the roots of the stonebreaker plant help stimulate bile production, aiding in the treatment of jaundice.
Leaves: High in flavonoids, stonebreaker leaves possess hepatoprotective properties that benefit jaundice patients by protecting the liver.
Flowers: Containing essential oils, stonebreaker flowers support digestion and have anti-inflammatory effects, useful for jaundice management.
Seeds: With high levels of alkaloids, stonebreaker seeds promote bile secretion and have a positive impact on liver function, aiding jaundice recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest phyllanthus niruri leaves and stems at dawn, ensuring freshness for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material with filtered water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Combine 100g of fresh phyllanthus niruri material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily meticulously.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with jaundice because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have a protective effect on the liver. It can help stimulate the production of bile, which is necessary for the breakdown and elimination of bilirubin, a yellow pigment that accumulates in the blood during jaundice. Additionally, licorice tincture has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver damage and promote healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using licorice tincture for jaundice, as it can interact with certain medications and may not be suitable for individuals with certain health conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, help reduce inflammation in the liver, a common symptom of jaundice.
Hepatoprotective: The hepatoprotective effects of licorice tincture, attributed to its flavonoid content, protect the liver from further damage, promoting recovery from jaundice.
Antioxidant: Licorice tincture's antioxidant properties, owing to its phenolic compounds, help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress in the liver, which is associated with jaundice.
Choleretic: Licorice tincture's choleretic action, due to the presence of glycyrrhetinic acid, stimulates the production of bile, aiding in the elimination of bilirubin, a key cause of jaundice.
Demulcent: The demulcent properties of licorice tincture, resulting from its mucilage content, soothe and protect the mucous membranes, alleviating discomfort and irritation associated with jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and liver-protecting properties, making it useful for treating jaundice.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain compounds that can help stimulate the production of bile, which can aid in the elimination of bilirubin, a compound that accumulates in the body during jaundice.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects and can help support liver function in cases of jaundice.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice plant are rich in saponins, which can help improve liver function and reduce the symptoms of jaundice.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains triterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and liver-protecting effects, making it useful for treating jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the fall season weighing approximately 500g.
Chop the harvested root material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped root with high-proof ethanol and distilled water in a 1:1 ratio by weight soaking for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter separating liquid tincture from solids discarding solids.
Transfer filtered tincture to dark glass bottles sealing tightly and storing at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ajwain tincture helps with jaundice because it contains therapeutic compounds that support liver function and bile production. The primary active ingredient in ajwain, thymol, has been shown to stimulate bile flow, which aids in the elimination of bilirubin from the body. Bilirubin is a yellow pigment that accumulates in the blood during jaundice, causing the skin and eyes to turn yellow. Additionally, ajwain's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help protect liver cells from damage, further supporting the body's efforts to manage jaundice. Overall, ajwain tincture offers a natural and effective way to alleviate jaundice symptoms and promote liver health.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue: Ajwain tincture stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin from the body. This property is attributed to the presence of thymol and limonene in ajwain.

Anti-inflammatory: Ajwain tincture reduces inflammation in the liver, which is beneficial for jaundice as it alleviates stress on the liver and promotes healing. The anti-inflammatory effects are primarily due to the presence of thymol and gamma-terpinene.

Carminative: Ajwain tincture aids in digestion and reduces gastric discomfort, which is important for jaundice as it helps alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. The carminative properties are attributed to the presence of thymol and eugenol.

Antioxidant: Ajwain tincture neutralizes free radicals and protects liver cells from damage, which is beneficial for jaundice as it supports liver function and promotes recovery. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the presence of thymol and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Ajwain tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, which is important for jaundice as it reduces the risk of infection and supports overall liver health. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of thymol and alpha-pinene.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Ajwain seeds are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for jaundice due to their high thymol content, which helps stimulate bile secretion and improve liver function.
Leaves: Ajwain leaves have hepatoprotective properties that can help protect the liver from damage and promote healing, making them useful for jaundice treatment.
Flowers: Ajwain flowers contain active compounds that can help improve digestion and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice.

Roots: Ajwain roots have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, making them a useful component in medicinal tinctures for jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ajwain leaves or dried ajwain flowers in bulk for making the tincture.
Clean and chop the harvested ajwain material into small pieces to increase its surface area.
Combine one part chopped ajwain with three parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily during preparation time.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after tincture has been prepared.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common fumitory tincture helps with jaundice because it stimulates the flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, which aids in the elimination of bilirubin, a pigment that accumulates in the blood and tissues, causing the yellowing of the skin and eyes characteristic of jaundice. The alkaloids in fumitory, such as fumaricine and protofumaric acid, contribute to this cholagogue effect.

Hepatic: Fumitory tincture has a tonic effect on the liver, promoting its overall health and function. This is crucial for jaundice, as it supports the liver in processing and eliminating bilirubin. The hepatic properties can be attributed to the alkaloids and flavonoids present in fumitory.

Diuretic: Common fumitory tincture increases urine production, which aids in the elimination of toxins and metabolic waste from the body. This is important for jaundice, as it helps remove bilirubin through the urine. The diuretic effect is attributed to the alkaloids and saponins found in fumitory.

This is essential for jaundice, as it helps maintain the liver's health and function during the healing process. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of common fumitory are used in making medicinal tinctures for jaundice due to their purifying and detoxifying properties.
Flowers: The flowers of common fumitory are used in medicinal tinctures for jaundice because of their ability to stimulate the liver and gallbladder.
Seeds: The seeds of common fumitory are used in medicinal tinctures for jaundice as they have a strong effect on the liver and bile ducts.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fumaria officinalis flowers and leaves when they are in bloom.
Clean and dry the fresh plant material by spreading it out on paper towels for several hours.
Chop the dried fumaria officinalis plant material into small pieces using scissors or a knife.
Combine 100 grams of chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for eight weeks, shaking the jar daily and filtering out the solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaundice with herbal tinctures is through the use of milk thistle, dandelion, and turmeric. Milk thistle, known for its liver-protecting properties, helps promote the regeneration of liver cells. Dandelion aids in bile secretion, which is crucial for breaking down bilirubin, the substance responsible for jaundice. Turmeric, with its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, assists in liver function improvement and bilirubin reduction. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaundice that are treated with herbal tinctures include liver disorders such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, and fatty liver disease. Milk thistle is a commonly used herb known to protect and repair the liver. Other helpful herbs include dandelion root, which aids in detoxification, and turmeric, which reduces inflammation. Artichoke leaf extract can also help stimulate bile production and improve liver function. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatments.
